name: mesher

metadata.name: mesher
metadata.summary: Mesher is a multi-objective unstructured mesh generation software.

documentation:
# mesher

Mesher is a novel multi-objective unstructured mesh generation software that allows mesh generation to be generated from an arbitrary number of hydrologically important features while maintaining a variable spatial resolution. Triangle quality is guaranteed as well as a smooth graduation from small to large triangles. Including these additional features resulted in a better representation of spatial heterogeneity versus classic topography-only mesh generation. The paper describing *mesher* can be [found here](https://research-groups.usask.ca/hydrology/documents/pubs/papers/marsh,_et_al_2018.pdf).

![](docs/source/images/mesher_veg.png)

### How to use
Detailed documentation is given [here](https://mesher-hydro.readthedocs.io).

### Install
Build requirements
  - Python >= 3.7
  - C++14 compliant gcc (>= gcc 7.3)
  - gdal >=3.8, cgal, boost, vtk>=9, metis

```
$ pip install mesher
```

or

```
$ conda install mesher
```

Detailed documentation on how to install is given [here](https://mesher-hydro.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation.html).




#### Spack
- Clone https://github.com/Chrismarsh/spack-repo
- Add `spack-repo` to spack `repos.yaml` https://spack.readthedocs.io/en/latest/repositories.html
- `spack install py-mesher`

modules.name: mesher.tools.mesh2shp
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation.append_global_cell_id_to_mesh_file
documentation: Read dictionary of arguments, find a desired permutation of cell faces, add new permuted ids to json file

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation.compute_rcm_permutation
documentation:
Computes the permutation that minimizes the bandwidth of the connectivity matrix
- uses Reverse CutHill-McKee (RCM) algorithm, which needs CSC or CSR sparse matrix format
permutation functions

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation.compute_nd_permutation
documentation: Computes a nested dissection permutation (minimizes fill-in of matrix factors)

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation.compute_metis_permutation_N
documentation:
Computes an nrank partition using METIS
- Uses metis deffault permutation for partitions

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation.convert_neighbor_list_to_compressed_matrix
documentation:
Get a sparse compressed_format from a list of neighbors (assuming aij input)
- compressed_format is one of sparse.csr_matrix or sparse.csc_matrix

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation.print_bandwidth_before_after_permutation
documentation: Display bandwidth before and after permutation
# Bandwidth comparison

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation.compute_bandwidth_CSR
documentation: Compute the maximum bandwidth from a CSR sparse matrix A

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshpermutation.plot_mat_connectivity
documentation:
Plot the spy view of the connectivity matrix
- TODO kwargs can be used to get values to pass into matplotlib functions

modules.name: mesher.tools.meshmerge
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshmerge.main
documentation: Read dictionary of arguments, find parameters, add new parameters to reference json file

functions.name: mesher.tools.meshstats.printProgress
documentation:
Call in a loop to create terminal progress bar
@params:
    iterations  - Required  : current iteration (Int)
    total       - Required  : total iterations (Int)
    prefix      - Optional  : prefix string (Str)
    suffix      - Optional  : suffix string (Str)
Print iterations progress
http://stackoverflow.com/a/34325723/410074
